title_en: Mona Khalil defied danger to protect her “family”
title_zh: 蒙娜·卡利尔不惧危险，守护她的“家人”
rubric_en: Lebanon’s most dedicated conservationist was killed on June 19th, aged 76
rubric_zh: 黎巴嫩最敬业的自然保护者于6月19日去世，享年76岁

paragraphs.en: It was the summer of 1999; and on a warm evening on Mansouri beach in southern Lebanon, Mona Khalil was strolling with a beer. She loved this beach. It was the world of her childhood holidays, running down to the rocky, sandy shore from her grandparents’ house. Her best summers had been spent here, adventuring. All the toys she had ever needed she had picked up here, and still did: weird stones, lengths of driftwood, coloured glass bottles. They now filled her house and hung clinking in the lemon and banana trees around it. This had become her dream life.
paragraphs.zh: 这是1999年夏天。在黎巴嫩南部的一次温暖傍晚，蒙娜·卡利尔（Mona Khalil）端着一杯啤酒在曼苏里（Mansouri）海滩散步。她热爱这片海滩。这里承载着她童年度假的世界，她从祖父母的家里跑下来，去到岩石与沙滩交界的海岸。她度过的最美好夏天都在这里度过，尽情冒险。所有她需要过的玩具都在这里捡到，而且一直都在：奇形怪状的石头、漂浮木、色彩斑斓的玻璃瓶。如今它们填满了她的房子，挂在周围的柠檬树和香蕉树间，轻轻碰撞作响。这便成了她理想的生活。

paragraphs.en: But suddenly on that evening she heard a noise, like a thud or a thump, quite close. The next thing she knew, someone was throwing sand at her. She looked down to see, not a dog or a fox, but a huge turtle digging a nest in which to lay her eggs. Later, when she had read more, she learned that this was a green sea turtle, a species that was critically endangered. They were herbivores, eating sea-grass and seaweed, and might live to 100 years old. This particular female would have swum hundreds, maybe even thousands, of miles to lay her eggs exactly here, where 30 years ago she had hatched. Unerring instinct had led her to Ms Khalil’s feet and then, her eggs laid and covered up, wearily back into the sea again. And all Ms Khalil could think of was how beautiful she was.
paragraphs.zh: 但就在那个晚上，她忽然听见一声很近的砰然声，像是重物落下。下一秒，她只觉有人在向她扔沙子。她低头一看，不是狗，也不是狐狸，而是一只巨大的乌龟在挖巢穴，为自己产卵。后来她读得更多后才知道，那是一只绿海龟，这个物种濒危程度极高。它们是草食动物，以海草和海藻为食，寿命可能长达一百年。那只雌龟可能游了数百英里，甚至几千英里，才准确地回到这里产卵——三十年前她自己就是在这里孵化的。毫不动摇的天性把她带到了卡利尔女士的脚边；待她把蛋产下并覆盖好后，疲惫地又回到大海。卡利尔女士脑中想到的只有：她多么美丽。

paragraphs.en: This was the point at which her life changed, and became only turtles. She did not talk much about the years before. School in Beirut, a marriage, and a son, Omar, killed at eight by a passing boat as he gazed at starfish underwater. An escape to the Netherlands during Lebanon’s civil war, where she stayed for ten years and worked as a porcelain restorer. Then, in 1999, the return with her friend, Habiba Fayed, to her grandparents’ house. It was falling down, swallowed in a forest of weakened trees. Together they put it to rights. She painted it orange as a homage to the Netherlands, and visitors noticed that Dutchness had touched her, too. A certain bluntness, an unLebanese reserve, a refusal to be swayed, and a dogged belief in just getting on with it.
paragraphs.zh: 就在这一刻，她的生活改变了，此后她几乎只关注乌龟。她很少谈起以前的岁月。贝鲁特的求学、婚姻，还有儿子奥马尔（Omar）：他当时在水下盯着海星时，被一艘驶过的船只撞死，年仅八岁。黎巴嫩内战期间，她逃往荷兰，在那里住了十年并做过瓷器修复师。然后在1999年，她与朋友哈比巴·法伊德（Habiba Fayed）一起返回祖父母的房子。那房子正摇摇欲坠，沉入一片病弱树木交织成的丛林中。两人一起把它修好。她把房子刷成了橙色，以此向荷兰致敬，来访者也注意到，荷兰也给她留下了影响：一种直接、带着非黎巴嫩式克制的坚定、一种不易被动摇的执拗，以及一种只管把事情做好就够了的执着。

paragraphs.en: “It” was a project, soon world-famous, to protect the turtles and their nests. It included carnivorous loggerhead turtles, also endangered and also users of Mansouri beach. Government, central or local, was prostrated by war and had no possible money to give. So she funded her training of new conservationists by turning the Orange House into a B&B. It was basic, with no air-conditioning, but it was also an oasis of calm, wind chimes, home-made jam—and serious research. The high point was when she would summon her volunteers to watch as she poured out a bucket of hatchlings on the sand and the tiny creatures, flippers going frantically, scurried down to the breaking waves.
paragraphs.zh: “它”后来成了一个很快闻名世界的项目：保护这些乌龟及其巢穴。还包括濒危的食肉龟类——棱皮海龟——它们同样使用曼苏里海滩。战争让中央和地方政府都濒临瘫痪，根本没有可支配的经费。于是她把“橙屋”改造成了一个简易民宿，以此资助自己培训新的保护者。那里虽然条件简朴，没有空调，却像一处绿洲般安静：风铃、手工果酱，以及认真的科研工作。最激动人心的时刻，是她召集志愿者观看那一幕：她把一桶刚孵出的幼崽倒在沙地上，小小的生命急切地扑打着鳍，慌忙向拍打岸边的浪潮奔去。

paragraphs.en: Their enemies were many. Dogs would dig up the nests to eat the eggs. Ghost crabs and birds ate the hatchlings, and plastic litter entangled them. Fishermen, working only just offshore, trapped adults in close-mesh nets and even disrupted their swims with dynamite. But the most insidious enemy was development. More and more flats, hotels and resorts were rising on either side of Mansouri; its unspoilt tranquillity was becoming rare. Security lights competed with the moon to confound the hatchlings’ sense of direction. Yet this small, hemmed-in site was the home to which the female hatchlings would one day feel compelled to return.
paragraphs.zh: 她的敌人很多。野狗会挖开巢穴去吃鸟蛋。寄居蟹和鸟类也会吃小海龟幼崽，塑料垃圾会缠住它们。渔民只在近岸作业时，就用细目网困住成年海龟，甚至用炸药惊扰它们的游动。然而最隐蔽而危险的敌人是开发。越来越多的浅滩、酒店和度假村在曼苏里两岸拔地而起，它原本原始而宁静的状态越来越稀有。安全灯光与月光竞争，混淆了幼龟的方向感。而这个又小又逼仄的地方，正是雌龟幼崽未来一生都会被“唤回”的家园。

paragraphs.en: She campaigned in all the ways she could think of. Before dawn she and her volunteers would patrol the shore, picking up litter and looking for fresh turtle tracks. She measured nests, roofed them with wire mesh, counted the papery-shelled eggs, helped the hatchlings out and sent her data to global conservation groups. After that, she fought. Passionately, sometimes tearfully, she repeated that the turtles had a right to the beach. It had been theirs for the past 250m years, before humans existed. Who were humans to kick them out? All they were asking for was a short season to nest. Then they, her family, would be gone again.
paragraphs.zh: 她尽她所能开展了各种倡议。每天黎明前，她和志愿者会巡查海岸，捡拾垃圾并寻找新鲜的海龟爬行痕迹。她测量每一个巢穴，用铁丝网覆盖，清点纸壳般的海龟蛋，帮助幼崽孵出并护送它们出海，然后把数据上报给全球保护组织。之后，她开始斗争。她带着热情，甚至带着泪水，一遍又一遍地重申：海龟有权利回到沙滩。那片海岸在过去的250万年里都属于它们，早在有人类之前就在那里。人类凭什么要把它们赶走？它们只要求一个短暂的产卵季节，然后，它们——她的“家人”——会再次离开。

paragraphs.en: Many people hated her incessant “No this, no that”. She understood: without work, without food, the last thing on their minds was ecology. Almost no Lebanese came to help her; only foreigners. Some locals even turned up with Kalashnikovs. But at least she made the fishermen give up using dynamite, and persuaded two local mayors to declare the beach a hima, a community reserve. She tried to restrict partying there, and stop cars driving onto it. She also did her best to obstruct the building of the Palagio Beach Resort almost next door, which contravened planning rules. And she petitioned the UN peacekeepers stationed in south Lebanon to stop throwing their rubbish into the sea.
paragraphs.zh: 许多人厌恶她那句句不断的“这里不行，那里也不行”。她理解这种情绪：没有工作、没有饭吃的时候，人们脑子里第一位想的不是生态保护。几乎没有黎巴嫩人来帮助她，来的人几乎全是外国人。甚至有些本地人还拿着喀什米尔式卡拉什尼科夫冲过来。幸运的是，她至少让渔民停止了使用炸药，并说服了两位地方市长宣布这片沙滩为hima，即社区保护区。她还试图限制这里的聚会活动，并阻止车辆驶上沙滩。她也尽力阻止几乎在隔壁兴建的Palagio Beach Resort，该项目明显违反了规划规定。她还向驻扎在黎巴嫩南部的联合国维和部队请愿，要求停止将垃圾丢进海里。

paragraphs.en: For war was the other constant consideration. The Orange House was only 12km inside the Israeli border. In 2006, when Israel attacked in nesting season, she asked Hizbullah fighters point-blank to leave the beach. They did, but then set up rockets right behind the house. She and Habiba had to flee, first to a hut on the beach, then, after the Orange House was hit, to Beirut. When they returned, it was to find a military checkpoint next door. In future, volunteers had to be cleared through security. Ironically, though, when war (or covid) emptied the beaches the turtles thrived. In 2007 she reported that around 5,000 hatchlings had successfully launched themselves.
paragraphs.zh: 而战争则是另一个始终存在的考虑。橘色小屋距离以色列边界仅12公里。2006年，以色列在产卵季节发动攻击时，她当面要求真主党战士离开这片沙滩。他们果然离开了，但随后在小屋后面布置了火箭阵地。她和哈比巴不得先逃到海滩上的一间小屋，之后在橘色小屋被击中后，又转往贝鲁特。等她们回到原处，门口却多了一个军事检查站。从那以后，志愿者必须经过安全放行才能进入。具有讽刺意味的是，当战争（或疫情）让海滩变得空荡荡时，海龟反而繁衍得更好。到2007年，她报告称，大约有5,000只幼崽成功冲出海岸。

paragraphs.en: Nonetheless, she made the resolution never to leave Mansouri beach again. No matter what, she would stay in her dream place. She made a statue for the garden out of all the Israeli shells that had already fallen on her house. Her dealings with Hizbullah (she had to; they were the local authority) possibly compromised her, but she waved that off. She was just a civilian. Anyway, there was no other heaven but her Orange House among the trees and by the beach. That was it. That was her patch, to which she had to return; as surely as the female turtles did, with unwavering determination, over mile after mile of rolling sea. ■
paragraphs.zh: 然而，她下定决心再也不离开曼苏里海滩。无论如何，她都会待在她的梦中之地。她用已经落在自己房子上的所有以色列炮弹残壳在花园里做了一个雕像。她与真主党的往来（她不得不如此；他们是当地当局）可能让她陷入危险，但她对此置之不理。她只是一名平民。无论如何，在树木和海滩之间，她的“橙屋”没有别的去处。这就是她的全部了。这就是她的地盘，她必须回到这里；就像雌性海龟那样，带着不动摇的决心，跨越一英里又一英里翻滚的海面。■
